title: Thamnocalamus tessellatus
taxonomy: Thamnocalamus tessellatus is a species of bamboo in the family Poaceae. It is classified in the genus Thamnocalamus. The species is further classified as a member of the tribe Bambuseae. Within this tribe, it belongs to the subtribe Bambusinae. It is also classified as a type of woody bamboo.

intro: Thamnocalamus tessellatus, commonly known as the striped bamboo, is a species of bamboo native to the Himalayas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Thamnocalamus tessellatus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
